definition: Three-candle pattern creating a gap (imbalance) where price moved too fast for balanced order flow.
depends_on: displacement (FVGs are CREATED BY displacement — no displacement, no meaningful FVG)
feeds_into: silver_bullet (silver bullet = FVG formed in specific time window)
consequent_encroachment (CE = 50% midpoint of FVG for precision entry)
related_to: displacement (FVG is the EVIDENCE of displacement)
premium_discount (FVG in wrong zone is less reliable)
bullish_FVG: Gap between candle 1 high and candle 3 low. Below current price.
bearish_FVG: Gap between candle 1 low and candle 3 high. Above current price.

consequent_encroachment.definition: The 50% midpoint of the FVG (CE) — the most precise entry point.
consequent_encroachment.connection: CE is the FVG's version of equilibrium (like dealing_range equilibrium). Price reaching CE and holding = FVG respected. Price closing through CE = FVG invalid.

timeframe_hierarchy: HTF FVGs (H4/D1) > LTF FVGs (M15/M5). A LTF FVG INSIDE a HTF FVG = highest probability. This nesting mirrors dealing_range nesting.

inversion_FVG.definition: FVG that was fully filled and traded through — now acts as opposite S/R.
inversion_FVG.connection: Inverted FVGs become OBSTACLES. If your trade path crosses an inverted FVG, reduce conviction.
